name: Search/canonical
description: Canonical URI (_profile) search conformance tests ported from fhir-server's CanonicalSearchTests.cs / CanonicalSearchTestFixture.cs. Three Observation fixtures carry distinctive meta.profile canonical references: obs-profile-v1 (uri|1#fragment), obs-profile-v2 (uri|2), and obs-profile-alt (an alternate uri plus uri|1 with no fragment). On R4/R4B/R5, servers commonly strip the version and fragment components from a canonical reference before indexing (Canonical => Uri), so an exact full-canonical search (uri+version+fragment) typically finds nothing, while a bare-uri search matches all resources that reference that uri at any version/fragment. Because _profile support is itself optional (the original suite skips entirely when the server does not advertise _profile as a supported Observation search parameter) and the exact canonical-stripping behavior is implementation-defined, every match/no-match assertion here is warningOnly; only the basic HTTP 200 / Bundle response shape is asserted as a hard requirement. The '#' character is percent-encoded (%23) in the query string per the original test's own comment that ASP.NET otherwise treats it as a URL fragment delimiter, not part of the query.

variable.name: profileAltId
variable.description: Id for obs-profile-alt (profiles: alternate uri, and uri|1 with no fragment)

test.name: _profile: full canonical (uri+version+fragment) search
test.description: Searching with the exact full canonical value (uri|1#fragment, '#' percent-encoded as %23) that obs-profile-v1 carries. On R4/R4B/R5, servers commonly strip the version/fragment before indexing (Canonical => Uri), so this exact-match search typically finds nothing.

assert.description: Search must return HTTP 200 (or 400 if _profile is not a supported search parameter on this server)

Should find no matches, since canonical version+fragment components are typically stripped before indexing (warningOnly: canonical-stripping behavior is implementation-defined, and STU3 servers may retain an exact match instead)

test.name: _profile: bare canonical uri search matches all resources referencing that uri at any version/fragment
test.description: Searching with just the bare uri (no version, no fragment) should match obs-profile-v1, obs-profile-v2, and obs-profile-alt (which references the same uri, at version 1, with no fragment, as its second profile entry) — since all three carry a profile whose uri component equals this value.

assert.description: Search must return HTTP 200 (or 400 if _profile is not a supported search parameter on this server)
Should include obs-profile-v1 (warningOnly: canonical-stripping behavior is implementation-defined)
Should include obs-profile-v2 (warningOnly: canonical-stripping behavior is implementation-defined)
Should include obs-profile-alt (warningOnly: canonical-stripping behavior is implementation-defined)

test.name: _profile: canonical uri+version (no fragment) search
test.description: Searching with uri|2 (version, no fragment) against obs-profile-v2's profile. On R4/R4B/R5, servers commonly strip the version before indexing, so this exact-match search typically finds nothing.

assert.description: Search must return HTTP 200 (or 400 if _profile is not a supported search parameter on this server)

Should find no matches, since the canonical version component is typically stripped before indexing (warningOnly: canonical-stripping behavior is implementation-defined, and STU3 servers may retain an exact match instead)

test.name: _profile: alternate canonical uri matches the observation referencing it
test.description: Searching with the bare alternate uri should match only obs-profile-alt, the sole fixture whose meta.profile includes that alternate uri.

assert.description: Search must return HTTP 200 (or 400 if _profile is not a supported search parameter on this server)
Should include obs-profile-alt (warningOnly: _profile search support is optional)
Should NOT include obs-profile-v1 or obs-profile-v2, which do not reference the alternate uri (warningOnly: _profile search support is optional)
